text:
To measure exactly 45 minutes using the ropes, we can follow these steps:

1. Light one rope at both ends. Since it takes 60 minutes to burn unevenly, lighting it at both ends will make it burn in 30 minutes.
2. At the same time, light the other rope at one end. 
3. When the first rope finishes burning (after 30 minutes), light the second rope at the other end as well. 
4. The second rope has been burning for 30 minutes, so it will take another 30 minutes to burn completely, but since we lit the other end, it will burn the remaining part in 15 minutes (half of the remaining 30 minutes).
5. So, when the second rope finishes burning, 45 minutes will have passed (30 minutes for the first rope + 15 minutes for the second rope).

The minimum number of ropes used is 2.

Verification checks:
1. Does the method account for the uneven burning
